articles.title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13’s distance record as humans travel farther from Earth than ever before
articles.description:
“Such a majestic view out here.”

summary: Artemis II surpasses Apollo 13's distance record, reaching unprecedented space travel milestones.

Boston residents can expect increased STEM opportunities as NASA promotes space-related initiatives.

articles.title: Mass. House to vote on youth social media ban, strict school cellphone ban
articles.description:
State lawmakers are supportive of the bans, while cities like Quincy are taking steps on their own to restrict cellphone use in schools.

The post Mass. House to vote on youth social media ban, strict school cellphone ban appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts House to vote on youth social media and cellphone bans on April 6.
Quincy and other cities are independently restricting cellphone use in schools.
Proposed bans aim to enhance student focus and mental health in Boston schools.

articles.title: How a blind man from Mass. made it possible for others with low vision to build Lego sets
articles.description:
Three years ago, Shifrin launched Bricks for the Blind. The 28-year-old now works with a team of 30 sighted writers and blind testers.

The post How a blind man from Mass. made it possible for others with low vision to build Lego sets appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Shifrin launched Bricks for the Blind three years ago to assist low-vision individuals.
The initiative employs 30 sighted writers and blind testers to create accessible Lego instructions.
This project enhances inclusivity for Boston residents with low vision, promoting engagement in creative activities.

articles.title: Maine father dies after saving his children from a rip current
articles.description:
The North Yarmouth man was vacationing in Florida when a current swept him and two of his children away from shore. Both children survived.

The post Maine father dies after saving his children from a rip current appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Maine father from North Yarmouth drowned saving his children from a rip current in Florida.
Both children survived the incident while vacationing on April 6, 2026.
Tragic event highlights dangers of rip currents for Boston families vacationing at beaches.

articles.title: He’s running the Boston Marathon to support neglected and at-risk kids
articles.description:
Sean Meehan is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for The Home For Little Wanderers.

The post He’s running the Boston Marathon to support neglected and at-risk kids appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Sean Meehan is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise funds for at-risk children.
Meehan supports The Home For Little Wanderers, a Boston organization aiding neglected kids.
Funds raised will directly benefit local children in need of support and resources.
